Smt. L.V. Government Polytechnic, Hassan has been shaping skilled engineers and proud citizens for over nine decades — through rigorous diploma education, hands-on workshops, and a campus community that feels like family.

Our Story

A Legacy Built Over Nine Decades

Established in 1934 under the scheme of the Directorate of Technical Education, Bengaluru, Smt. L.V. Government Polytechnic began as a modest technical institute on B.M. Road, Hassan. In 1957, the institution formally introduced diploma-level engineering courses, opening doors for thousands of students from Hassan district and beyond.

Today, spread across a 27-acre campus, the college offers diploma programmes in Mechanical, Civil, Electrical & Electronics, Automobile and Computer Science & Engineering, supported by well-equipped laboratories, a library housing thousands of volumes, separate hostels for boys and girls, and a vibrant alumni network — the Smruthi Mandara Alumni Association — that continues to give back to the campus that shaped them.

1934

Founded under the Directorate of Technical Education, Bengaluru, as a technical training institute serving Hassan district.

1957

Began offering full diploma programmes in engineering, marking the institution's transition into a full-fledged polytechnic college.

1970s – 1990s

Expanded across Mechanical, Civil, Electrical & Electronics, and Automobile Engineering, with new laboratories and workshop blocks added to the 27-acre campus.

2000s

Introduced the Computer Science & Engineering programme and modernised computing and digital laboratories for a new generation of students.

Today

Home to over a thousand students, an active Smruthi Mandara Alumni Association, and a continuing commitment to skilled, industry-ready engineering education.

Newsroom

June 2026

Admissions Open for Diploma Batch 2026–27

Applications are now open for first-year diploma seats across all five engineering branches.

May 2026

CSE Department Hosts AI & ML Workshop

A two-day hands-on workshop introduced students to the fundamentals of artificial intelligence and machine learning.

April 2026

Robotics Team Wins State-Level Competition

Students from the Mechanical and Electrical departments secured first place in the Karnataka State Robotics Challenge.

March 2026

New Computer Lab Inaugurated

A fully upgraded computer laboratory was inaugurated to support the Computer Science & Engineering programme.

Terms of Use & Intellectual Property

By accessing this website, you agree to comply with the following strict Terms of Use. Any breach will result in legal action:

  • Proprietary Code & Assets: All design layouts, custom stylesheets, compiled scripts, watermarked images, and logos are the sole intellectual property of the developers and Smt. L.V. Government Polytechnic. Duplication, mirroring, or redistribution of any asset is strictly forbidden.
  • Protection Bypass Forbidden: Attempting to inspect code, bypass image protection overlays, disable copy barriers, or scrape website content using automated tools or DevTools is a direct violation of these terms.
  • Form Submission & Security: Submissions via the Admission Enquiry or registration forms must be accurate and authentic. Flooding, spamming, injection attacks, or submitting fraudulent information will result in immediate IP banning.
  • Limitation of Liability: The developers and the institution are not liable for any temporary server downtimes, data loss from form submissions, or issues arising from external links.
  • Legal Enforcement: We reserve the right to monitor site access and pursue strict legal remedies under cybersecurity, data protection, and copyright laws for any unauthorized duplication or security compromise.
